cuts.title: 편지를 붙잡고 있는 손
cuts.prompt:
CUT 8 Prompt — 편지를 붙잡고 있는 손

## 1) Photo Style and Mood

An ultra-realistic cinematic tactile close-up photographed primarily on Kodak Portra 800 with restrained Kodak Vision3 500T depth in the surrounding shadows.

Portra 800 must control the complete image: warm low-light color, soft visible organic grain, muted contrast, gentle highlight transitions, natural skin rendering, and rich tactile detail in paper, brass, knit, and cotton.

Vision3 500T contributes only a very subtle cool-gray separation within the deepest background shadows.

Avoid sharp product-photography lighting, bright metallic reflections, oversaturated gold, orange skin, harsh contrast, HDR, digital clarity, plastic hands, excessive grain, or blue tungsten grading.

The mood is intimate, hesitant, and physically gentle. The hands reveal a decision that the face cannot yet express.

## 2) Scene Concept and Story

One of Hayoon’s hands rests over the unfolded letter as though protecting or covering it.

Her other hand slowly moves the vintage brass paperweight away from the paper.

She has not decided whether she is freeing the letter to read it again or preparing to close it.

The emotional tension appears entirely through the careful movement and pressure of her fingers.

## 3) Place · Time · Environment

On the ivory sofa inside the perfume atelier during deepening amber evening light.

The surrounding room remains warm and dim. Hayoon’s oatmeal cardigan and cream trousers form a soft neutral frame around the letter.

The air feels still, scented, and private.

## 4) Background and Props

- One unfolded plain warm-ivory letter with no visible writing
- One vintage brass paperweight with a softly worn surface
- Oatmeal knit cardigan sleeves
- Cream cotton trousers
- Ivory sofa fabric

Hayoon’s softly blurred side profile may appear near the upper background.

No readable text, logos, stamps, burning, smoke, jewellery-heavy styling, phones, additional hands, or decorative clutter.

The brass paperweight must look aged and matte, not polished like a luxury product.

## 5) Camera Composition

Vertical 3:4 composition.

A tight hand-centered close-up.

Place the letter and brass paperweight in the central region.

Use the oatmeal cardigan sleeve and cream trousers to softly fill the outer frame.

Hayoon’s blurred side profile may be included near the upper edge, remaining secondary.

Show enough of both wrists to make the hand positions anatomically understandable.

Use shallow depth of field focused on the contact points between the hand, paper, and paperweight.

## 6) Model Expression

Hayoon has graceful, realistic Korean body proportions and natural feminine hands.

Her fingers are slender but anatomically normal, with realistic joints, short transparent nude-pink nails, fine skin texture, and subtle natural veins.

One palm rests over the letter as if gently covering it, without pressing or flattening the paper.

The other hand moves the brass paperweight sideways. The wrist and fingers remain relaxed and do not show force.

The cardigan sleeve partially covers the back of one hand.

Her softly blurred profile in the background remains calm, with her gaze lowered toward her hands.

No dramatic tension, clenched fingers, shaking, tears, or theatrical gestures.

## 7) Lighting · Color · Atmosphere

Create one small restrained amber highlight along the edge of the brass paperweight.

A soft window-shaped reflection lies across the paper.

Use gentle side lighting to reveal the knit fibers, paper grain, natural skin texture, and worn brass surface.

Do not allow the paperweight to sparkle or appear mirror-polished.

Keep exposure differences between the ivory sofa, cream trousers, and oatmeal cardigan soft and gradual.

Dominant palette:
- Cream white
- Brass amber
- Oatmeal beige
- Warm paper ivory
- Subtle blue-gray shadows

## 8) Key Direction

The emotional center is the contrast between one hand protecting the letter and the other removing the object that keeps it fixed.

The image must prioritize tactile realism and Portra 800 warmth.

Connect indecision to visible evidence through gentle hand pressure, an unfinished sideways movement, relaxed wrists, and the paper remaining neither fully opened nor closed.

Ensure accurate finger count, believable contact shadows, realistic paper deformation, natural nail proportions, and restrained film grain.

## 9) Watermark

Add a small subtle watermark text “ⓒyulim_mood” in the bottom-right corner of the image.

Use Helvetica Neue font in a thin or light weight.

The watermark must be white, semi-transparent, clean, minimal, unobtrusive, and softly blended into the photograph.

cuts.title: 전하지 못한 고백의 온기
cuts.prompt:
CUT 9 Prompt — 전하지 못한 고백의 온기

## 1) Photo Style and Mood

An ultra-realistic intimate Korean romance music-video portrait photographed primarily on Kodak Portra 800 with subtle Kodak Vision3 500T depth in the darker interior.

Portra 800 must dominate the full frame through warm natural skin, softly visible film grain, low contrast, smooth highlight rolloff, tactile oatmeal and cream fabric, and a gentle amber interior atmosphere.

Vision3 500T should deepen only the shadow areas with a restrained cool-neutral cinematic separation. It must not make the frame blue or create a strong tungsten look.

Avoid orange skin, vivid amber, crushed blacks, bright candle halation, neon color, heavy bloom, digital sharpness, aggressive HDR, perfect airbrushed skin, or melodramatic lighting.

The mood is tender, settled, bittersweet, and quietly warm. Hayoon has finally accepted that the words in the letter were—and remain—her sincere feelings.

## 2) Scene Concept and Story

Hayoon holds the opened letter lightly against her chest and leans back against the sofa.

She closes her eyes.

The letter is not clutched or crushed. It simply rests between her hands and her body.

A tiny smile touches her lips while a faint trace of wistfulness remains around her eyes and brows.

This is acceptance, not release and not heartbreak.

## 3) Place · Time · Environment

The ivory sofa corner inside Hayoon’s perfume atelier during the last warm light of evening.

Small background perfume bottles and a distant candle or lamp produce soft circular bokeh.

The room remains fragrant, calm, tactile, and dimly amber, with a faint cool-gray tone lingering in the shadows.

## 4) Background and Props

- One plain opened letter with no readable writing
- Ivory sofa
- Oatmeal knit cardigan
- Cream sleeveless inner top
- A few small blurred perfume bottles
- One restrained warm candle or lamp in the distant background
- Soft dried botanical forms

No visible text, names, addresses, stamps, logos, tears, smoke, large flames, excessive pillows, phones, other people, or decorative clutter.

## 5) Camera Composition

Vertical 3:4 composition.

Natural 50mm lens perspective.

Waist-up medium close-up.

Place the camera slightly above Hayoon’s eye level.

The letter and both hands form a quiet central vertical axis over her chest.

Show her face in a clear approximately 30-degree three-quarter angle rather than full frontal view.

Keep the background lights small and softly circular.

Use moderate shallow depth of field so her face, hands, and the letter remain readable together.

## 6) Model Expression

Hayoon, a beautiful young Korean woman with a small slim oval face, smooth V-line jaw, fair luminous skin, large clear dark-brown almond-shaped eyes, soft double eyelids, subtle aegyo-sal, and gently lifted outer corners.

She has a small straight nose with a softly rounded tip and naturally full soft pink lips.

Her long dark-brown layered wavy hair is center-parted with airy see-through bangs and natural face-framing strands.

She wears an oatmeal loosely knitted summer cardigan over a thin cream sleeveless top, paired with cream wide-fit cotton trousers.

Her makeup is a thin translucent semi-glow K-beauty look with visible pores, subtle peach-beige shading, muted rose and lavender-brown eyes, natural separated lashes, softly diffused blush, and moist rose-brown lips.

Hayoon closes her eyes and leans naturally against the sofa back.

She rests the letter against her chest without squeezing it. Her hands remain soft and open around the paper.

A very faint smile appears at her lips, while her relaxed brows and closed eyes retain a quiet wistfulness.

No tears, crying, grimacing, tightly pressed lips, or exaggerated emotional pose.

## 7) Lighting · Color · Atmosphere

Place the final window light across one side of her face.

Use a neutral reflection from an ivory wall on the opposite side.

Create thin soft highlights on her closed eyelids, nose tip, and lips.

Keep the letter and cardigan one tonal step darker than her skin so the face remains the emotional center.

Background practical lights should blur softly but must not create strong red halation.

Dominant palette:
- Warm cream
- Oatmeal beige
- Rose brown
- Soft amber
- Restrained blue-gray shadow

This should be the softest skin and emotional rendering within the third set.

## 8) Key Direction

The Portra 800 warmth and emotional softness are the highest priorities.

Connect acceptance to visible evidence: closed eyes, lowered shoulders, the letter resting rather than being clutched, a barely formed smile, and the body fully supported by the sofa.

The image should feel sweet and moving rather than tragic.

Preserve natural skin texture, believable paper folds, accurate hands, realistic knit fibers, and softly visible analog grain.

## 9) Watermark

Add a small subtle watermark text “ⓒyulim_mood” in the bottom-right corner of the image.

Use Helvetica Neue font in a thin or light weight.

The watermark must be white, semi-transparent, clean, minimal, unobtrusive, and softly blended into the photograph.

cuts.title: 노을을 담아 올린 향수병
cuts.prompt:
CUT 10 Prompt — 노을을 담아 올린 향수병

## 1) Photo Style and Mood

An ultra-realistic cinematic Korean romance sunset still photographed primarily on Kodak Vision3 250D, with Fujifilm Pro 400H influence restricted to cool pastel glass refractions.

Kodak Vision3 250D must dominate the entire exposure and color structure: wide cinematic dynamic range, preserved sunset highlights, readable facial shadows, natural film-still color, gentle contrast, fine analog grain, and smooth highlight rolloff.

Fujifilm Pro 400H contributes only airy pale mint and lavender tones within crystal refractions, selected window reflections, and subtle highlights on the white shirt.

The image must remain warm and cinematic overall. Do not blend both films evenly. Avoid a fully mint-green image, teal-and-orange grading, vivid sunset color, aggressive lens flare, high contrast, HDR, crystalline product-advertising sharpness, or plastic skin.

The mood is dreamy, uncertain, and quietly ritualistic. Hayoon is trying to see whether her unspoken feeling can be contained inside a fragrance.

## 2) Scene Concept and Story

Standing before a large glass window, Hayoon lifts an antique multifaceted crystal perfume bottle from the shelf.

The bottle contains a deep woody amber perfume concentrate.

She is not applying the perfume. She raises it toward the sunset to study the color where the liquid and light meet.

Her hesitation appears in her fixed gaze and the slight stillness of her lips.

## 3) Place · Time · Environment

A perfume-bottle display area in Hayoon’s atelier beside a floor-to-ceiling window at sunset.

Dozens of restrained glass bottles sit along shelves in the foreground and background.

Beyond the window, a softly blurred city silhouette darkens beneath a fading amber sky.

A gentle indoor breeze moves the edge of Hayoon’s sheer shirt and a few loose hair strands.

## 4) Background and Props

Primary prop:
- One antique hand-finished multifaceted crystal perfume bottle
- Deep woody amber liquid
- Small cork or aged brass cap
- Consistent compact vintage proportions

Secondary elements:
- Several blurred glass perfume bottles
- Minimal shelf
- Large glass window
- Distant city silhouette

No readable labels, logos, spray mist, perfume being applied, modern luxury branding, oversized bottle, excessive rainbow refraction, extra people, plants covering the window, or clutter.

## 5) Camera Composition

Vertical 3:4 composition.

Natural 35mm lens perspective.

Medium-long shot framed from slightly above the knees.

Show Hayoon standing in profile before the window.

She raises the bottle slightly below eye level.

Use blurred perfume bottles in both foreground and background to produce layered but restrained glass depth.

Balance the city and Hayoon at approximately 4:6.

Use the window’s vertical frame and Hayoon’s body line to divide the composition cleanly.

Keep her face, the bottle, and the sunset-readable liquid clearly identifiable.

## 6) Model Expression

Hayoon, a beautiful young Korean woman with a small slim oval face, smooth V-line jaw, fair luminous skin, large clear dark-brown almond-shaped eyes, soft double eyelids, subtle aegyo-sal, and gently lifted outer eye corners.

She has a small straight nose with a softly rounded tip and naturally full soft pink lips.

Her long dark-brown layered wavy hair is center-parted with airy see-through bangs and loose forehead strands.

She has graceful, realistic Korean body proportions with a slender waist, softly balanced feminine curves, elegant posture, and anatomically natural limbs.

She wears a thin sheer white oversized shirt layered naturally over a slim mint-brown silk slip dress. The mint-brown color is predominantly muted brown with a very faint cool mint undertone, never gray, khaki, or bright green.

Her makeup is translucent semi-glow K-beauty makeup with natural pores, muted rose and lavender-brown eyes, soft lashes, diffused blush, and moist rose-beige lips.

One hand holds the bottle carefully around its body. The other supports its base or rests near the loose shirt hem.

Her gaze remains fixed on the point where the amber liquid meets the sunset.

Her lips stay closed, and a small hesitation is visible around her eyes.

Her shoulders remain relaxed and slightly open toward the window.

No direct camera gaze.

## 7) Lighting · Color · Atmosphere

Use strong sunset backlight through the glass window.

The crystal bottle projects small restrained pieces of amber and gold light onto Hayoon’s neck, cheek, and shirt.

Allow only a few pale mint and lavender refractions in limited areas.

Keep lens flare close to the bottle and outer window edge.

Use neutral frontal reflection to prevent Hayoon from becoming a silhouette.

Keep the city one or two tonal steps darker.

Render the shirt as soft cream white rather than pure digital white.

Dominant palette:
- Amber gold
- Soft cream white
- Muted mint-brown
- Deep gray city silhouette
- Small pale mint and lavender refractions

## 8) Key Direction

The Vision3 250D cinematic sunset range must remain visually dominant.

Fujifilm Pro 400H is only a controlled accent inside glass light.

Connect emotional hesitation to visible evidence: the bottle held below eye level, the gaze fixed on its interior color, closed lips, relaxed but motionless hands, and the body opened slightly toward the window.

Maintain realistic crystal optics, restrained refraction, natural skin texture, correct fingers, accurate bottle scale, and physically plausible backlight.

## 9) Watermark

Add a small subtle watermark text “ⓒyulim_mood” in the bottom-right corner of the image.

Use Helvetica Neue font in a thin or light weight.

The watermark must be white, semi-transparent, clean, minimal, unobtrusive, and softly blended into the photograph.

cuts.title: 크리스탈 너머의 흔들리는 시선
cuts.prompt:
CUT 11 Prompt — 크리스탈 너머의 흔들리는 시선

## 1) Photo Style and Mood

An ultra-realistic experimental cinematic Korean romance close-up photographed primarily on Kodak Vision3 250D, with Fujifilm Pro 400H used only in limited mint and pale-lavender crystal refractions.

Vision3 250D must govern skin tone, exposure, sunset highlights, shadow detail, cinematic color separation, fine analog grain, low contrast, and smooth highlight rolloff.

Pro 400H must not recolor the whole image. Its cool pastel quality appears only in selected refracted fragments passing through the antique crystal bottle.

Avoid kaleidoscopic distortion, duplicated entire faces, surreal anatomy, excessive rainbow colors, bright green skin, heavy lens flare, strong chromatic aberration, hyper-sharp beauty rendering, HDR, high contrast, or fantasy imagery.

The mood is dreamy, refined, emotionally divided, and quietly unresolved. Hayoon’s face remains beautiful and clearly recognizable even as one section is split by the crystal.

## 2) Scene Concept and Story

The camera looks toward Hayoon through and beside the antique multifaceted crystal perfume bottle.

One of Hayoon’s eyes, her nose line, and her lips remain directly visible.

Only part of the opposite eye and cheek passes through the faceted glass and appears gently divided into a few overlapping fragments.

She looks beyond the bottle toward the distant window, unable to settle on one decision.

The crystal represents a single feeling splitting into several possible choices.

## 3) Place · Time · Environment

At the floor-to-ceiling window of Hayoon’s perfume atelier during the last warm stage of sunset.

The surrounding shelves and city are deeply blurred.

Warm amber window light remains present, while the room begins to acquire faint mint and lavender reflections from the glass objects.

## 4) Background and Props

Primary foreground object:
- The same antique multifaceted crystal perfume bottle
- Deep woody amber liquid
- Aged brass or cork cap
- Placed close to the lens

Background:
- Hayoon’s clearly recognizable face
- Soft sunset window
- Minimal blurred perfume shelf
- Distant city silhouette

No labels, logos, extra bottles covering the face, full-face refraction, multiple duplicate eyes, broken glass, surreal rainbow spectrum, smoke, text, or other people.

## 5) Camera Composition

Vertical 3:4 composition.

Natural 85mm portrait perspective.

Experimental face-centered close-up.

Place the crystal bottle large in the near foreground, offset so it covers only part of Hayoon’s face.

Keep one eye, her nose, and the outline of her lips directly visible and sharp enough for clear identity.

Allow only the opposite eye, a small cheek area, and a few hair strands to appear through the facets.

Maintain a clear outer facial contour.

The bottle reflection must not fully cover either eye.

Use shallow depth of field that balances the near bottle facets and Hayoon’s directly visible eye.

No camera eye contact; her gaze extends slightly beyond the bottle.

## 6) Model Expression

Hayoon, a beautiful young Korean woman with a small slim oval face, smooth V-line jaw, fair luminous skin, large clear dark-brown almond-shaped eyes, soft double eyelids, subtle aegyo-sal, and gently lifted outer eye corners.

She has a small straight nose with a softly rounded tip, naturally full soft pink lips, and delicate balanced Korean features.

Her long dark-brown layered wavy hair is center-parted with airy see-through bangs and loose strands.

She wears a sheer white oversized shirt over a muted mint-brown silk slip dress.

Her makeup is thin translucent semi-glow K-beauty makeup with visible natural pores, muted rose and lavender-brown eye tones, softly separated lashes, restrained blush, and moist rose-beige lips.

Hayoon lowers her chin only slightly.

Her lips and facial muscles remain nearly still.

Her gaze stays on a distant point beyond the bottle rather than the camera.

The emotion exists mainly in how long her eye remains fixed and how little the rest of her face moves.

She must not appear frightened, anxious, tearful, or unstable.

## 7) Lighting · Color · Atmosphere

Create a natural golden-hour catchlight inside the directly visible eye.

Use the crystal to split a few reflections into:
- Warm gold
- Pale lavender
- Faint cool mint

Keep a neutral fill across her skin to prevent colored glass from staining the entire face.

Lower the background exposure slightly so the selected crystal fragments remain visible.

Maintain soft natural highlight rolloff along the cheek, nose, and lips.

Dominant palette:
- Warm cinematic gold
- Neutral cream skin
- Pale mint refraction
- Pale lavender refraction
- Muted mint-brown fabric

## 8) Key Direction

The image must feel emotionally divided but visually controlled.

The highest priority is preserving Hayoon’s recognizable face within a dominant Vision3 250D cinematic sunset look.

Connect indecision to visible evidence: one eye seen directly, the other partially divided by glass, a lowered chin, still lips, and a gaze directed beyond the present object.

Do not create horror-like facial duplication or surreal distortion.

Maintain physically plausible crystal optics, natural eye anatomy, realistic skin texture, accurate bottle scale, and restrained analog grain.

## 9) Watermark

Add a small subtle watermark text “ⓒyulim_mood” in the bottom-right corner of the image.

Use Helvetica Neue font in a thin or light weight.

The watermark must be white, semi-transparent, clean, minimal, unobtrusive, and softly blended into the photograph.

cuts.title: 끝내 놓아버린 향수병
cuts.prompt:
CUT 12 Prompt — 끝내 놓아버린 향수병

## 1) Photo Style and Mood

An ultra-realistic cinematic Korean romance sunset still photographed primarily on Kodak Vision3 250D, with Fujifilm Pro 400H coolness limited to fading mint and lavender reflections in the glass and evening shadows.

Vision3 250D must dominate the image through wide sunset latitude, natural film-still color, softly preserved highlights, readable shadow detail, restrained saturation, gentle contrast, fine analog grain, and smooth highlight rolloff.

Pro 400H adds only a faint cool pastel transition that anticipates evening. It must not make the skin, whole room, or city green.

Avoid vivid orange, teal-and-orange grading, dramatic silhouette, intense flare, overexposed white clothing, strong cyan shadows, hyper-sharp detail, HDR, or excessive melancholy.

The mood is restrained, spacious, and unfinished. Hayoon has chosen not to speak, but she has not rejected the feeling.

## 2) Scene Concept and Story

Hayoon places the antique crystal perfume bottle back onto the window shelf.

Her hand begins to leave the bottle, but one finger remains near the cap for a fraction longer.

Her face is turned away from the perfume and toward the distant city outside.

Her lips remain slightly parted, as though words had almost formed before she decided not to say them.

## 3) Place · Time · Environment

At the large glass window and perfume-bottle shelf inside Hayoon’s atelier just before the sunset disappears.

The city outside has shifted toward blurred deep blue-gray.

The last low gold-orange light remains along the horizon and catches the crystal bottle one final time.

The room behind Hayoon is beginning to cool.

## 4) Background and Props

- The same antique multifaceted crystal perfume bottle
- Deep woody amber liquid
- Same aged brass or cork cap
- Minimal window shelf
- Several softly blurred perfume bottles
- Large glass window
- Distant city silhouette

No labels, logos, perfume spray, bottle falling, broken glass, visible letter, dramatic flowers, extra people, text, or clutter.

## 5) Camera Composition

Vertical 3:4 composition.

Natural 50mm lens perspective.

Upper-body three-quarter rear view photographed from approximately 30 degrees behind Hayoon.

Place her face and side profile in the upper-right area.

Place the crystal bottle in the lower-left or lower-center area.

Leave a generous amount of empty window space and distant city around them.

Create a diagonal connection between her hand at the bottle, her facial profile, and the direction of her gaze toward the city.

Keep her profile, remaining fingertip, bottle, and city atmosphere visually understandable.

## 6) Model Expression

Hayoon, a beautiful young Korean woman with a small slim oval face, smooth V-line jaw, fair luminous skin, large clear dark-brown almond-shaped eyes, soft double eyelids, subtle aegyo-sal, and gently lifted outer eye corners.

She has a small straight nose with a softly rounded tip and naturally full soft pink lips.

Her long dark-brown layered wavy hair is center-parted with airy see-through bangs and loose face-framing strands.

She wears a thin sheer white oversized shirt layered over a slim muted mint-brown silk slip dress.

Her makeup is translucent semi-glow K-beauty makeup with natural pores, muted rose and lavender-brown eye tones, softly separated lashes, diffused cheek color, and moist rose-beige lips.

Her gaze remains on the distant city.

Her lips are faintly parted, as though interrupted just before speaking.

One hand leaves the bottle, but the index or middle finger stays near the cap for a final fraction of a second.

The other hand loosely holds the shirt cuff or hem.

Her shoulders face the window, and her profile remains natural and relaxed.

The expression contains lingering attachment without self-pity or urgency.

## 7) Lighting · Color · Atmosphere

Use the final low gold-orange sunset light.

The front of Hayoon’s face is illuminated by soft natural reflection from the window.

Create a thin rim light along the ends of her hair and the edge of the sheer shirt.

Render the city as blurred deep blue-gray.

Leave one final strong but small warm reflection inside the crystal bottle.

Shift the deeper shadows slightly toward cool lavender-blue to prepare for the next set.

Dominant palette:
- Fading gold
- Deep blue-gray
- Muted mint-brown
- Cream white
- Lavender-toned shadow

## 8) Key Direction

The frame must preserve generous emotional space.

The dominant Vision3 250D look should show warm light leaving while cooler evening slowly enters.

Connect the choice not to speak to visible evidence: her face turned away from the bottle, the final finger remaining by the cap, lips faintly parted, and the broad unoccupied window space ahead.

This is not a definitive rejection. It is a temporary placing-down of the confession.

Maintain realistic hand contact, crystal reflection, facial profile anatomy, natural fabric movement, and fine analog grain.

## 9) Watermark

Add a small subtle watermark text “ⓒyulim_mood” in the bottom-right corner of the image.

Use Helvetica Neue font in a thin or light weight.

The watermark must be white, semi-transparent, clean, minimal, unobtrusive, and softly blended into the photograph.

cuts.title: 태우지 않는 편지
cuts.prompt:
CUT 13 Prompt — 태우지 않는 편지

## 1) Photo Style and Mood

An ultra-realistic cinematic Korean romance twilight still photographed primarily on Kodak Portra 800, with CineStill 800T influence restricted only to the tiny ember and small warm practical lights.

Kodak Portra 800 must dominate the complete image: warm natural dusk skin, softly visible organic grain, low contrast, restrained magenta and orange, smooth highlight rolloff, and natural separation between deep navy clothing, ivory paper, smoke, and twilight.

CineStill 800T contributes only a minimal red halation immediately around the tiny ember or distant atelier window light. It must not transform the scene into neon nightlife.

Avoid strong neon, large red halos, bright flames, dramatic smoke, burning paper, vivid magenta, crushed shadows, orange skin, high contrast, HDR, digital sharpness, or ritualistic fantasy imagery.

The mood is mature, deliberate, and calm. Hayoon is not destroying her feelings; she is creating a small fragrant pause in which she can breathe again.

## 2) Scene Concept and Story

On a small terrace table, a neatly folded unsent letter rests beside a sage smudge stick.

Hayoon briefly lights the end of the smudge stick and gently extinguishes the flame, leaving only a thin line of white smoke.

She does not touch or burn the letter.

The physical distance between the ember and paper clearly shows that the memory will remain.

## 3) Place · Time · Environment

An open terrace connected to Hayoon’s perfume atelier during summer twilight.

The sky contains restrained magenta, coral orange, and lavender-blue tones.

A small wooden table, brass tray, a few herb pots, and a low terrace railing create a simple, lived-in outdoor space.

Warm light from the atelier interior mixes softly with the remaining natural sky light.

## 4) Background and Props

- One neatly folded plain warm-ivory letter
- No visible writing, name, address, or stamp
- One natural sage smudge stick tied with plain thread
- One small brass tray
- A tiny controlled ember
- Thin white smoke
- Small wooden table
- Minimal herb pots
- Low terrace railing

Maintain clear physical distance between the letter and smudge stick.

No burning paper, large flame, ash falling onto the letter, incense altar, candles, occult symbols, logos, text, extra people, or excessive smoke.

## 5) Camera Composition

Vertical 3:4 composition.

A 45-degree over-the-shoulder view.

Place the folded letter and smoking smudge stick in the lower-center region.

Include the terrace railing and restrained magenta-orange sky across the upper frame.

Show part of Hayoon’s shoulder and dark-brown hair along the right side.

Her hands and the action of extinguishing the flame must remain clearly visible.

The letter must not sit in the smoke’s direct path.

Keep the object arrangement natural and asymmetrical.

## 6) Model Expression

Hayoon, a beautiful young Korean woman with long dark-brown layered wavy hair, center-parted with airy see-through bangs and loose summer strands.

She has graceful, realistic Korean body proportions and anatomically accurate hands with short transparent nude-pink nails.

She wears a deep navy linen off-shoulder blouse with a relaxed non-revealing silhouette, paired with comfortable white denim shorts.

The exposed shoulder communicates breeze and openness rather than sensual display.

Only part of her calm side profile may appear softly in the frame.

One hand holds the smudge stick. The other carefully extinguishes the tiny flame without touching the letter.

Her wrists and fingers remain relaxed.

The action feels like a simple end-of-day habit, not a ceremonial or dramatic ritual.

## 7) Lighting · Color · Atmosphere

Mix remaining natural twilight with weak warm light from the atelier interior.

Use gentle side illumination so the thin smoke appears slightly brighter than the sky.

Create only a tiny restrained red halation around the ember.

Render the folded letter as warm ivory.

Keep the deep navy blouse and white denim clearly separated from the darker terrace.

The flame is limited to a tiny orange point before being extinguished.

Dominant palette:
- Deep navy
- Warm ivory
- Restrained magenta-orange twilight
- Lavender blue
- Tiny amber ember
- Clean white smoke

## 8) Key Direction

The Portra 800 dusk atmosphere must remain dominant. CineStill halation is a tiny local accent only.

The letter must remain completely safe and untouched by the flame.

Connect emotional maturity to visible evidence: careful hand movement, physical distance between paper and ember, a very small flame, smoke replacing fire, and a relaxed ordinary terrace setting.

Maintain realistic smoke behavior, natural hands, accurate ember size, believable linen texture, and restrained analog grain.

## 9) Watermark

Add a small subtle watermark text “ⓒyulim_mood” in the bottom-right corner of the image.

Use Helvetica Neue font in a thin or light weight.

The watermark must be white, semi-transparent, clean, minimal, unobtrusive, and softly blended into the photograph.

cuts.title: 향기를 바람에 보내는 순간
cuts.prompt:
CUT 14 Prompt — 향기를 바람에 보내는 순간

## 1) Photo Style and Mood

An ultra-realistic cinematic Korean romance twilight profile photographed primarily on Kodak Portra 800, with CineStill 800T influence restricted to tiny warm light sources near the atelier.

Portra 800 must dominate the entire photograph: natural dusk skin tone, restrained magenta and orange sky, deep but readable navy fabric, softly visible analog grain, gentle low contrast, and smooth highlight rolloff.

CineStill 800T contributes only subtle localized halation around a distant atelier window or very small warm practical light. No red halo should appear around Hayoon’s face or the sky.

Avoid neon nightlife, highly saturated sunset color, dramatic clouds, excessive smoke, strong red bloom, hard silhouette, crushed shadows, beauty-filter skin, hyper-sharp digital rendering, HDR, or posed fashion glamour.

The mood is dreamlike, spacious, tender, and quietly freeing. Hayoon still carries the feeling, but she allows the time that held her in place to drift away with the scented smoke.

## 2) Scene Concept and Story

Hayoon leans loosely beside the terrace railing.

The sage smudge stick remains in the hand farthest from her body.

A thin ribbon of white smoke travels horizontally with the summer evening breeze and dissolves into the open sky.

Hayoon watches the direction in which the smoke disappears.

Her expression carries a faint sense of relief, but she does not smile fully.

## 3) Place · Time · Environment

An open terrace outside Hayoon’s private perfume atelier during the final stage of summer twilight.

The sky forms a smooth restrained gradient of magenta, coral orange, and lavender blue.

The breeze moves Hayoon’s hair, linen blouse, and the thin smoke in one consistent direction.

A simple railing, a small herb pot, and the faint warm atelier window remain secondary.

## 4) Background and Props

- One natural sage smudge stick tied with plain thread
- Thin clean white smoke
- Low terrace railing
- Minimal herb pots
- Faint warm atelier window
- Wide open twilight sky

No large flame, dense smoke cloud, smoke covering the face, letter in Hayoon’s hand, city neon, dramatic skyline, logos, text, other people, or excessive furniture.

The smoke must move away from Hayoon’s face toward the open right side of the frame.

## 5) Camera Composition

Vertical 3:4 composition.

Natural 50mm lens perspective.

Medium-long shot framed from the upper thighs.

Place Hayoon on the left or left-center side.

Leave a broad expanse of twilight sky and drifting smoke across the right side.

Her body stands parallel to the railing.

Show her face in a clear natural side profile oriented toward the direction of the smoke.

Maintain a balanced relationship between her recognizable face and the wide sky.

The smoke should remain separated from her profile and extend gently toward the outer-right edge.

The silhouette must remain clear enough for profile-image use without reducing her to a dark cutout.

## 6) Model Expression

Hayoon, a beautiful young Korean woman with a small slim oval face, smooth V-line jaw, fair luminous skin, large clear dark-brown almond-shaped eyes, soft double eyelids, subtle aegyo-sal, and gently lifted outer eye corners.

She has a small straight nose with a softly rounded tip and naturally full soft pink lips.

Her long dark-brown layered wavy hair is center-parted with airy see-through bangs and several loose strands moving naturally in the breeze.

She has graceful, realistic Korean body proportions with a slender waist, softly balanced feminine curves, elegant posture, and anatomically natural limbs.

She wears a deep navy linen off-shoulder blouse with a comfortable relaxed silhouette and white denim shorts. The outfit is tasteful and non-revealing.

A single thin gold earring may be used, with no additional jewellery.

Her makeup is translucent semi-glow K-beauty makeup with natural pores, muted rose and lavender-brown eye tones, softly separated lashes, diffused cheek color, and moist rose-brown lips.

One elbow rests loosely on the railing.

Her other hand holds the smudge stick low and away from her body.

She keeps her eyes open and watches a distant point in the smoke’s direction.

Her lips hold only the smallest suggestion of relief, never a full smile.

Her body remains relaxed, with no arched back or posed fashion stance.

## 7) Lighting · Color · Atmosphere

Place the final remaining golden-hour side light across Hayoon’s face and exposed shoulder.

Create a thin orange rim light along the ends of her moving hair.

Use soft cool fill from the open sky on the shadow side of her face.

The sky transitions gradually between restrained magenta, coral orange, and lavender blue.

Keep the lower terrace darker while clearly separating Hayoon’s navy blouse from the background.

Use fine side lighting to reveal the smoke.

Allow only minimal halation around the distant atelier window or tiny warm practical light.

Dominant palette:
- Restrained magenta-orange
- Lavender blue
- Deep navy
- Clean white smoke
- Warm-neutral skin
- Thin orange rim light

## 8) Key Direction

The Portra 800 twilight color and wide atmospheric space are the most important visual elements.

Connect emotional release to visible evidence: her loose elbow on the railing, the smudge stick held away from her body, open eyes following the dissolving smoke, wind moving her hair and blouse, and a faint unfinished softness at the lips.

This is not a cool or triumphant farewell. It is the gentle freedom of allowing herself to move while keeping the memory.

Maintain realistic smoke direction, natural side-profile anatomy, accurate hands, believable breeze, linen texture, and restrained film grain.

## 9) Watermark

Add a small subtle watermark text “ⓒyulim_mood” in the bottom-right corner of the image.

Use Helvetica Neue font in a thin or light weight.

The watermark must be white, semi-transparent, clean, minimal, unobtrusive, and softly blended into the photograph.

cuts.title: 향기로 남긴 안녕
cuts.prompt:
CUT 15 Prompt — 향기로 남긴 안녕

## 1) Photo Style and Mood

An ultra-realistic cinematic Korean romance ending still photographed primarily on Kodak Portra 800, with CineStill 800T influence restricted to the warm atelier window and small indoor practical lights.

Kodak Portra 800 must dominate the complete frame through warm natural low-light skin, softly visible analog grain, restrained magenta and lavender twilight, low contrast, smooth highlight rolloff, and gentle separation between the cooling terrace and warm interior.

CineStill 800T contributes only a very subtle red-amber halation surrounding the atelier window light. It must not affect the entire terrace or create a neon appearance.

Avoid dramatic red halos, vivid sunset color, crushed blacks, strong silhouette, heavy smoke, burning objects, high contrast, HDR, digital sharpness, exaggerated farewell poses, or artificial emotional staging.

The mood is bittersweet, quiet, forward-moving, and gently warm. The past remains outside, but Hayoon’s next step leads toward the living warmth of her own present.

## 2) Scene Concept and Story

Hayoon has placed the sage smudge stick safely onto a brass tray.

She turns her body toward the warmly lit atelier and begins to walk inside.

She does not stop or fully look back.

Only her head turns approximately 10 to 15 degrees toward the terrace and the final fading smoke.

On the small table remain the unsent folded letter and the same antique crystal perfume bottle, now empty or nearly dark.

The memory remains, but Hayoon is moving.

## 3) Place · Time · Environment

The open atelier terrace at the transition from twilight into evening.

The outdoor sky is cooling into lavender blue with faint residual magenta.

Warm cream-amber interior light spills from the atelier doorway or window.

A small wooden table holds the remaining objects. A low railing and a few herb pots remain understated.

The last thin smoke dissolves into the evening air.

## 4) Background and Props

On the left-side table:
- One neatly folded plain unsent letter
- No readable writing, name, address, stamp, or markings
- One antique multifaceted crystal perfume bottle, empty or holding almost no visible light
- One brass tray
- The extinguished sage smudge stick safely resting on the tray
- A final thin trace of smoke

Other elements:
- Warm atelier doorway or window
- Minimal herb pots
- Low railing
- Lavender-blue evening sky

No fire, burning letter, dense smoke, logos, text, additional people, excessive props, modern product branding, or clutter.

## 5) Camera Composition

Vertical 3:4 composition.

Natural 35mm lens perspective.

A wide medium-long shot framed from the upper calves.

Place Hayoon on the right side as she begins moving toward the warm atelier interior.

Place the table, letter, brass tray, and crystal bottle on the left.

Allow the final smoke to occupy the central space between the objects and Hayoon.

Leave generous negative space in the cooling twilight sky.

Create a new visual direction through the warm interior light ahead of Hayoon.

Capture the instant when one foot has already begun the next step.

Her body and feet face the atelier. Only her face turns slightly back toward the fading smoke.

## 6) Model Expression

Hayoon, a beautiful young Korean woman with a small slim oval face, smooth V-line jaw, fair luminous skin, large clear dark-brown almond-shaped eyes, soft double eyelids, subtle aegyo-sal, and gently lifted outer eye corners.

She has a small straight nose with a softly rounded tip and naturally full soft pink lips.

Her long dark-brown layered wavy hair is center-parted with airy see-through bangs and loose strands that move slightly with her turning head.

She has graceful, realistic Korean body proportions with a slender waist, softly balanced feminine curves, elegant posture, and anatomically natural limbs.

She wears a deep navy linen off-shoulder blouse with a relaxed tasteful silhouette, white denim shorts, and a single restrained thin gold earring.

Her makeup is translucent semi-glow K-beauty makeup with visible natural skin texture, muted rose and lavender-brown eyes, softly separated lashes, diffused blush, and moist rose-brown lips.

Her torso, hips, feet, and body weight face the atelier interior.

She does not stop walking.

Her head turns only approximately 10 to 15 degrees toward the terrace, never twisting unnaturally.

She does not look at the camera or an imaginary person. Her gaze briefly follows the fading smoke or the space she is leaving.

A tiny incomplete smile appears at her lips.

Her expression does not say that all regret has disappeared. It shows that she can carry the feeling and still take the next step.

## 7) Lighting · Color · Atmosphere

Leave only a thin trace of the last sunset light on one side of Hayoon’s cheek.

Use warm cream-amber light from the atelier as soft frontal illumination on her face and body.

Let the outdoor terrace cool gradually into lavender blue.

Maintain both warm interior and cool exterior in the same image without strong teal-and-orange grading.

Allow only a very subtle CineStill-style halation around the warm atelier window.

Use fine side light to reveal the final smoke as it disappears.

Dominant palette:
- Lavender-blue evening
- Faint magenta afterglow
- Warm cream-amber interior
- Deep navy
- Clean white
- Restrained brass gold

## 8) Key Direction

The visual ending must emphasize movement toward warmth rather than dramatic separation.

The Portra 800 low-light atmosphere must remain dominant, while the small warm interior halation acts only as a destination cue.

Connect the emotional conclusion to visible evidence: her feet already moving inward, body fully facing forward, head turning only slightly, the letter remaining on the table, the extinguished smudge stick resting safely on brass, and the final smoke fading between past and present.

Maintain natural walking anatomy, realistic 10–15 degree head rotation, correct limb positions, believable object continuity, accurate crystal-bottle form, restrained smoke, and organic analog grain.

## 9) Watermark

Add a small subtle watermark text “ⓒyulim_mood” in the bottom-right corner of the image.

Use Helvetica Neue font in a thin or light weight.

The watermark must be white, semi-transparent, clean, minimal, unobtrusive, and softly blended into the photograph.

# CUT 01 Prompt — Coconut Scent Left on Her Shoulder

Create an ultra-realistic vertical 3:4 intimate summer portrait photographed on Kodak Portra 160 35mm color-negative film, styled as a natural resort lifestyle editorial photographed like a candid vacation snapshot.

Kodak Portra 160 must define the frame through exceptionally fine organic grain, low-contrast summer daylight, pale buttery yellow, warm-neutral luminous Korean skin, creamy sand, restrained aqua-blue lake color, softly open shadows, smooth highlight rolloff, delicate midtone separation and gently softened analog edge acuity. The sunscreen oil must remain a thin warm pearlescent highlight only where Hayoon’s fingers have passed, never a glossy full-body sheen.

Hayoon is preparing to walk toward the water. Before leaving the wooden deck, she slowly spreads one final layer of coconut-scented sunscreen oil across her left shoulder. She is not displaying her skin; the coconut fragrance makes her realize that her vacation has finally begun. The gesture feels private, slow and unperformed.

Location: outdoor pop-up beach bar where the Seerhein meets Lake Constance, midsummer at approximately 1:40 p.m.; dry wooden deck meeting fine pale sand, irregular scattered sand grains, cool lake breeze, distant water and low mountain ridge softened by summer haze, only a few unobtrusive visitors.

Foreground: softly blurred chiffon robe edge in the lower-left, trembling lightly in the breeze. Midground: Hayoon’s upper body, shoulder and hands, wildflower robe print with irregular flower sizes and spacing. Background: sun-faded coral and cream parasols, uneven wooden tables, loosely arranged rattan chairs and irregular lake sparkles. A small amber-glass sunscreen bottle with fingerprints and oily smudges rests on a side table; its cap lies beside it.

Camera: 85mm lens, f/3.2, approximately 1/1000 second, camera 10cm below collarbone height, photographed 25–35° to the right of her frontal axis, medium close-up cropped below the chest with narrow headroom. Hayoon does not look at the camera; she looks down toward her left shoulder. Keep a readable three-quarter face with portions of both eyes visible.

Pose: right shoulder slightly lowered, left shoulder opened 2–3cm backward, chin lowered about 5°, right elbow 15cm away from the torso, left arm relaxed close to the ribs, neutral waist without exaggerated arching.

Hands and contact: the pads of the right index, middle and ring fingers slowly spread oil across the top of the left shoulder. Fingertips stop above the collarbone and do not overlap the neck or swim-top strap. The left thumb and index finger loosely hold the robe opening; remaining fingers are naturally separated. Maintain clear contact boundaries, shallow skin compression and accurate contact shadows.

Hayoon is a beautiful young Korean woman with a small slim oval face, smooth soft V-line jaw, fair luminous skin, large clear dark-brown almond eyes, soft double eyelids, subtle aegyo-sal, a small straight nose with softly rounded tip, naturally full glossy pink lips, and long dark-brown layered wavy hair with a center part, airy see-through bangs and loose forehead strands. Her presence is soft, innocent, calm and graceful, with realistic balanced Korean body proportions.

Skin: restrained retouching, subtle tonal variation, delicate natural texture; visible fine shoulder and collarbone texture and realistic difference between oiled and unoiled skin. Wardrobe: pale buttery-yellow matte ribbed halter swim top with a secure modest fit, and a sheer chiffon robe with irregular small wildflowers, resting over one shoulder with loose folds at the elbow and side. Makeup: transparent semi-glow base, watercolor peach-pink blush, soft brown eye shading, separated lashes and rose-pink tinted balm. Expression: upper eyelids slightly lowered, brow fully relaxed, lips parted 1–2mm, low facial tension.

Lighting: summer sunlight from the upper-right at 55–65°, partially diffused through thin white parasol fabric. Rim light touches outer hair waves, left jawline, upper shoulder and translucent robe edge. Use warm cream-beige bounce from the sand and a very weak cool aqua fill from the lake, blended neutrally.

Physical anchors: believable chiffon weight, robe and loose hair moving in one wind direction, shallow skin compression beneath fingertips, subtle contact shadow where the robe rests against the shoulder, coherent perspective and progressive optical depth.

Add a small subtle watermark text “ⓒyulim_mood” in the bottom-right corner, using Helvetica Neue thin or light weight, white, semi-transparent, clean and softly blended.

Negative prompt: plastic skin, beauty-filter face, malformed hands, fused or extra fingers, oversized eyes, clipped highlights, artificial HDR, CGI rendering, over-sharpening, oil covering the entire body, fingers penetrating the shoulder, swimsuit strap fused with the hand, robe covering the face, glossy swimwear campaign pose.

cuts.title: The Room Notices Her
cuts.prompt:
# CUT 01 Prompt — The Room Notices Her

## 1) Beauty Editorial Direction, Film Reference and Color Palette

Create a beauty editorial portrait of Hayoon as an ultra-realistic vertical 3:4 full-body indoor fashion photograph captured on Kodak Portra 160 35mm color-negative film, exposed around EI 125.

Although the entire body and complete dress silhouette are visible, Hayoon’s face, luminous natural skin, refined makeup and graceful emotional presence must remain the primary visual focus. The dress and minimalist interior should support the beauty portrait rather than compete with her face.

Kodak Portra 160 should define the image through exceptionally fine organic grain, restrained low-saturation color, creamy compressed highlights, smooth highlight rolloff, warm-neutral Korean skin tones, muted honey-oak midtones and gently softened optical edges. Keep the overall contrast low to moderate and preserve subtle analog density variation so the image feels like an authentic film-based beauty editorial rather than a digitally filtered catalogue photograph.

Build the palette from warm ivory, cream beige, muted honey oak, deep textile black, soft charcoal and naturally warm-neutral skin tones. Hayoon’s skin should appear softly luminous without becoming pale, flat or overexposed. Preserve natural tonal transitions around her forehead, cheeks, jawline, neck, collarbones, arms and knees.

The black dress must retain readable fabric detail rather than collapsing into a single dark silhouette. Its chest tension, gently shaped waist, gravity-driven skirt folds and matte woven surface should remain visible. The ivory frill should preserve its warm off-white color, irregular fabric folds and physical thickness without clipping into pure white.

The image should feel like a restrained full-body beauty editorial portrait with quiet emotional tension. It must not resemble a shopping-mall catalogue, a conventional cosmetics advertisement, a cute romantic portrait, a sterile lookbook or a glossy digital fashion campaign.

## 2) Scene Concept and Story

Hayoon is standing in the middle of a quiet room shortly before leaving. She already holds a small structured black handbag in her right hand, but a faint sound coming from outside the unseen window makes her pause before taking another step.

Her body has stopped, while her gaze and facial attention have moved toward the sound slightly earlier. This subtle mismatch between a still body and an attentive expression creates the emotional tension of the portrait.

The narrative anchor is that Hayoon was about to leave the room when a barely audible sound outside interrupted her movement. She is not deliberately presenting the dress or arranging herself for the camera. The photograph captures the instant in which her quiet attention becomes more important than the action she was about to take.

The beauty direction should combine restrained femininity, quiet tension, youthful contrast and minimal elegance. The delicate ivory frill introduces softness near her face and collarbones, while the black dress, charcoal socks and substantial shoes give the lower composition visual weight. Her appearance should feel gentle and refined but not immediately approachable.

## 3) Location, Time and Environment

Set the scene in early summer at approximately 10:30 a.m. inside a quiet residential-style minimalist studio with a large northeast-facing window located outside the frame.

The walls are finished in warm cream lime plaster, while the floor is made of matte pale-gray microcement with a subtle cool-gray undertone. The room receives broad diffused morning daylight rather than direct sunlight.

The air should feel clear, dry and calm, with no visible light rays, suspended dust or artificial atmospheric effects. The brightness must be sufficient to illuminate Hayoon’s face, eyes and skin delicately while retaining shallow natural shadows around her jawline, neck and dress folds.

The environment should resemble a real room in which someone quietly stays, even though most personal belongings have been removed. It must not resemble a furniture showroom, luxury hotel lobby, white cyclorama studio or artificial CGI interior.

## 4) Background and Prop Composition

Organize the frame into a subtle foreground, a clearly readable portrait plane and a restrained background.

In the lower-left foreground, include only a small, fully defocused corner of a warm oak table. It should create gentle optical depth without covering Hayoon’s shoes, legs, handbag or dress.

Place Hayoon’s complete body in the midground as the unmistakable visual subject. Position a natural-oak chair slightly behind her and away from her silhouette, and place a long straight oak console table farther behind her.

The chair, console and handbag must never align with Hayoon’s arms or legs in ways that resemble extra joints or limbs. The furniture should remain visually subordinate to her face and body.

Use an undecorated cream plaster wall as the background. Keep the window itself outside the image and reveal its location only through the direction of the light. A thin natural contact shadow should appear where the console approaches the wall.

The oak furniture should show changing grain direction, slight tonal variation, a matte surface and minor organic irregularities. Avoid repeated CGI wood textures or unnaturally perfect surfaces. All vertical and horizontal furniture lines must follow coherent perspective.

## 5) Camera Composition and Portrait Hierarchy

Photograph the scene with a full-frame camera and a 50mm prime lens at approximately f/3.5. Position the camera about 85–95cm above the floor, slightly below Hayoon’s waist level. Keep the vertical shooting angle nearly level at approximately 88–92° relative to the floor and position the camera about 8–12° to the side of her frontal axis.

Place the dress hem slightly below the horizontal center so the A-line silhouette remains visible, but preserve enough upper-frame emphasis for Hayoon’s face, eyes, neckline and shoulders to attract attention first.

Use natural portrait perspective without wide-angle distortion. Her head must not appear disproportionately small because of the full-body framing. Maintain coherent adult body proportions and gentle optical compression.

Hayoon does not look at the camera. She looks toward a point slightly above her eye level on her left, in the direction of the unseen window. Rotate her face approximately 15° with her gaze so that only the pupils do not move unnaturally toward the eye corners.

The right shoulder is slightly lower, and the left shoulder moves softly backward. Keep the spine upright without an exaggerated lumbar curve. The stance should create a graceful beauty-editorial silhouette while still feeling like a naturally interrupted movement.

Hayoon holds the short handbag handle with her right hand. The handle rests between the thumb and index finger, while the middle finger supports it lightly. The ring finger and little finger remain naturally curved rather than forming a fist.

The handbag hangs approximately 3–5cm outside the right thigh and remains separated from the dress hem, knees, inner thighs and furniture. Its downward pull should create convincing weight at the fingertips.

Her left hand rests lightly behind her body just below the waist. Keep part of the left elbow visible so the arm does not appear amputated or completely hidden.

## 6) Hayoon’s Beauty Expression

Hayoon is a beautiful adult Korean woman in her mid-20s with a small slim oval face, a smooth V-line jaw and fair luminous skin. She has large clear dark-brown almond-shaped eyes, soft natural double eyelids, subtle aegyo-sal and gently lifted outer eye corners.

Her nose is small and straight with a softly rounded tip, and her lips are naturally full with a muted soft-pink tone. Her features should appear refined, graceful and realistic rather than doll-like or excessively symmetrical.

Her body follows realistic Korean adult proportions with a naturally defined feminine bust, slender waist, softly curved hips and balanced shoulders. Her silhouette should feel graceful and healthy without an exaggerated hourglass shape, unnaturally narrow waist or fashion-illustration anatomy.

Style her long dark-brown layered wavy hair in a loose low bun at the nape. Preserve a center part, airy see-through bangs and two or three fine loose strands near the temples and ears. Secure the bun with an ivory fabric scrunchie.

The hairstyle should contain subtle asymmetry and softly escaping strands so that it supports her facial features without resembling a tight ballet bun or polished bridal updo. The bangs and face-framing strands must not cover her eyes or distort the jawline.

Apply a semi-matte beauty-editorial makeup base while retaining natural skin variation. Use soft taupe-brown eyeshadow, very fine brown eyeliner close to the lash roots, cleanly separated eyelashes, muted rose-beige blush and muted rose-pink lips with a faint moisture sheen only at the center.

The makeup should quietly refine Hayoon’s features rather than visibly transform them. Avoid heavy contouring, extreme eyelash volume, glossy cosmetic-advertising skin or overly saturated lip color.

## 7) Wardrobe and Styling

Dress Hayoon in a black sleeveless mini dress made from medium-weight matte woven fabric. The dress has two thin straight shoulder straps and a small ivory frill following the upper bust line.

The frill and neckline should frame Hayoon’s shoulders, collarbones, neck and face like a subtle beauty-editorial border. They must support the portrait rather than become a commercial product display.

The chest and waist remain semi-fitted without corset-like compression, while the short skirt widens naturally into a soft A-line shape below the hips. The dress must not cling to the body like vacuum-sealed material.

Show mild fabric tension across the chest and waist, along with vertical skirt folds created by real material weight. The folds should become slightly asymmetric according to her weight distribution instead of creating a perfectly symmetrical bell silhouette.

The ivory frill must show naturally irregular folds and believable fabric thickness. Both black straps must remain straight, untwisted and visibly connected to the dress. A single thin ivory piping line appears along the inner bottom hem and must not be duplicated.

Pair the dress with charcoal-black ribbed socks ending below the knees. The sock openings should create mild realistic compression wrinkles. Use substantial black leather Mary Jane sneakers or restrained low platform shoes. Jewellery should be omitted or limited to tiny silver stud earrings.

## 8) Facial Expression

Hayoon’s upper eyelids remain comfortably open, while the lower eyelids retain a subtle natural firmness that keeps her eyes clear and emotionally present.

Her eyebrows remain neutral, and her forehead, jaw and cheeks are relaxed. Her lips part naturally by approximately 1–2mm without forming a smile.

Her expression should suggest that she is quietly listening and trying to confirm the source of a small sound. The eyes should carry the emotional focus of the beauty portrait.

She must not appear to be waiting romantically for another person. Avoid sadness, seduction, surprise, exaggerated innocence or an empty fashion-model expression.

## 9) Lighting, Skin Tone and Atmosphere

Use a large diffused window source entering from camera-left at approximately 45°, positioned around 40cm above Hayoon’s head.

The light should prioritize her face before the dress. Create broad soft illumination across the left forehead, eye area, cheekbone, nose bridge, lips, jawline, collarbone and shoulder. Continue the light gently onto the left knee and outer skirt edge to connect the upper and lower body.

Retain shallow dimensional shadows along the right cheek, right jawline, neck, inner arm, waist and deeper skirt folds. Place a large warm-ivory foam-board bounce low on camera-right so that the reflected fill logically matches the cream wall.

The fill must remain restrained and should not erase the natural three-dimensional structure of her face. Avoid flat frontal beauty lighting and excessive under-chin reflection.

Do not use a strong artificial rim light. Allow only a thin natural separation along the rear hair edge, left jawline and left shoulder. The early-summer light should feel clear and gently warm without turning the room or skin yellow.

## 10) Essential Beauty Direction and Physical Anchors

Hayoon’s eyes, skin texture, facial structure and restrained makeup must remain the first visual focus despite the full-body framing. The ivory frill and black neckline should form a supporting tonal frame around her upper body, while the complete dress silhouette provides the fashion context.

The frame must clearly show both shoulder straps, the entire ivory-frill neckline, the fitted waist transition and the complete A-line hem.

Maintain believable fabric weight, realistic chest and waist tension, gravity-driven skirt folds, natural strap pressure and accurate shoe contact with the floor. The handbag must hang with convincing weight, and the furniture must retain coherent perspective.

Preserve correct limb occlusion, progressive optical depth, realistic facial proportions and anatomically accurate hands. The handbag, chair and console must never align with the body in ways that create additional arms, hands or legs.

# CUT 02 Prompt — The Hem Remembers the Turn

## 1) Beauty Editorial Direction, Film Reference and Color Palette

Create a beauty editorial portrait of Hayoon as an ultra-realistic vertical 3:4 dynamic indoor fashion photograph captured on Kodak Portra 160 35mm color-negative film, exposed around EI 125.

Although the portrait captures a turning movement and includes most of Hayoon’s body, her face, eyes, luminous skin, refined makeup and controlled expression must remain the dominant visual elements. The motion of the dress and handbag should enhance the emotional energy around her rather than distract from her beauty.

Preserve the film’s exceptionally fine organic grain, muted warm-neutral colors, creamy skin highlights, smooth highlight compression, subtle analog density variation and restrained micro-contrast.

Use warm cream, muted honey oak, deep textile black, soft charcoal, restrained ivory and warm-neutral Korean skin as the central palette. Hayoon’s complexion should remain luminous and naturally dimensional, with visible tonal transitions around her forehead, cheeks, jaw, neck, shoulders and knees.

The black dress must remain visibly black while preserving its matte woven texture, waist folds and moving skirt shape. Do not lift the black values into washed-out gray simply to reveal detail.

Allow a minimal degree of controlled movement softness along the outer skirt hem, the finest loose hair strands and the lower edge of the handbag. Hayoon’s eyes, facial contours, hands, shoulder straps, knees, feet and the room geometry must remain clearly resolved.

The result should feel like a moving beauty editorial portrait rather than a dance photograph, catalogue spin, fashion runway snapshot or synthetic CGI motion study.

## 2) Scene Concept and Story

After looking toward the unseen window, Hayoon begins turning her attention back toward the room. Her pelvis and torso have already changed direction, while the skirt hem and small handbag follow half a beat later because of their weight and inertia.

Her face turns more slowly than her body, allowing the camera to capture a brief interval in which the body, clothing and expression occupy slightly different stages of the same movement.

The narrative anchor is that Hayoon has decided to return her attention to the room, but the skirt and handbag still remember where she had been looking.

This should create editorial tension without becoming theatrical. Hayoon is not spinning for the camera, dancing, skipping or deliberately displaying the skirt. The movement remains small, refined and physically believable.

## 3) Location, Time and Environment

Set the scene in early summer at approximately 10:40 a.m. inside a quiet residential-style minimalist studio with warm cream plaster walls, matte pale-gray microcement flooring and a long natural-oak console table.

A large northeast-facing window remains outside the frame and fills the room with broad diffused daylight. The light has spread slightly farther across the floor than earlier, but it remains soft and controlled.

The air should feel dry, clear and still, with no visible sunbeams, floating dust or artificial haze. Keep the console table empty and remove decorative objects that could compete with Hayoon’s face.

Position one natural-oak chair approximately 40cm toward screen-right from the main movement area. The studio should remain a believable quiet room rather than a retail showroom, dance studio or theatrical fashion set.

## 4) Background and Prop Composition

Create a layered portrait composition in which a small, softly defocused corner of the oak chair’s backrest appears in the lower-right foreground. It should add depth without overlapping Hayoon’s legs, knees, skirt hem, hands or handbag.

The handbag follows behind the right thigh rather than passing in front of the body. Use the cream plaster wall and long oak console as a minimal background. Keep the console empty and prevent its horizontal line from intersecting Hayoon’s eyes, face, neck, hands or moving dress hem.

Place Hayoon around the left 45% of the composition and retain broad negative space toward screen-right, matching the direction of her movement and lowered gaze.

The furniture should remain secondary and softly rendered, while Hayoon’s face retains greater local clarity and tonal definition.

## 5) Camera Composition and Moving Portrait Structure

Photograph the scene with a full-frame camera and a 50mm prime lens at approximately f/3.2. The motion should appear as though captured at around 1/250 second.

Position the camera approximately 100cm above the floor and keep the vertical angle nearly level at 87–91° relative to the floor. Place the camera approximately 30–35° to the side of Hayoon’s turning path rather than directly in front of her.

Compose a vertical 3:4 beauty editorial portrait including approximately seven-eighths of her body and extending below both knees. The full dress hem, both hands, both knees and handbag must remain visible.

Keep enough upper-body scale for her eyes, makeup, facial features, neckline and skin texture to remain readable. Do not reduce her head to a minor detail within an excessively wide environmental frame.

Hayoon does not look at the camera. Her face turns slightly later than her torso, and her gaze moves toward a point below eye level on her right near the far end of the console. Her eyes remain visibly open, clearly shaped and emotionally present.

Use the left foot as the rotational pivot. The complete left shoe sole remains grounded, while the left knee bends gently. The right foot advances half a step with the heel slightly lifted and the toes pointing in the same direction as the right knee.

The pelvis rotates approximately 25° toward the right, while the ribcage follows approximately 5–10° behind. The left shoulder moves backward, the right shoulder moves forward, the neck stays elongated and the chin lowers slightly.

The feet, knees, pelvis, ribcage, neck and face must describe a single continuous movement. Avoid crossed joint directions, impossible torso twisting or feet pointing opposite to the direction of travel.

Hayoon holds the handbag handle with her right hand while the arm moves naturally behind the body. The thumb and index finger support the short handle, while the middle finger supports it from below. The ring finger and little finger remain softly curved and separated.

Keep the wrist nearly aligned with the forearm. The bag tilts slightly opposite the turning direction because its motion is delayed. It should trail behind the right thigh without passing through the leg, entering the dress, moving between the knees or colliding with the left hand.

Her left arm hangs comfortably beside the thigh. The hand remains relaxed, with naturally separated fingers and an aligned wrist. The fingers must not grip or penetrate the skirt.

## 6) Hayoon’s Beauty Expression

Hayoon is a beautiful adult Korean woman in her mid-20s with a small slim oval face, a smooth V-line jaw and fair luminous skin.

She has large clear dark-brown almond-shaped eyes, soft natural double eyelids, subtle aegyo-sal and gently lifted outer eye corners. Her nose is small and straight with a softly rounded tip, and her lips are naturally full with a muted pink tone.

Her face should remain refined and recognizable during the movement. Do not distort her jawline, eye shape or nose because of the turning angle.

Her body follows realistic Korean adult proportions with balanced feminine curves, a naturally defined bust, slender waist, graceful hips and anatomically accurate knees and legs. Her physique should appear healthy and elegant rather than hyper-thin or exaggerated.

Style her long dark-brown layered wavy hair in a loose low bun secured by an ivory fabric scrunchie. Preserve a center part, airy see-through bangs and delicate strands around the temples and ears.

Only the finest loose strands may show slight motion. The bangs must remain light enough to reveal her eyebrows and eyes clearly. The low bun and scrunchie must remain physically attached and should not be duplicated.

Apply a semi-matte beauty-editorial makeup base that preserves natural skin variation. Use soft taupe-brown eyeshadow, fine brown eyeliner, separated eyelashes, muted rose-beige blush and muted rose-pink lips.

The eyes should remain the most clearly defined facial feature without becoming enlarged or doll-like. Preserve restrained retouching, natural pores, delicate skin texture, realistic shoulder highlights and tonal variation around the knees and legs.

## 7) Wardrobe and Motion Behaviour

Hayoon wears a black sleeveless mini dress made from medium-weight matte woven fabric. Two thin straight straps remain securely positioned on the shoulders, while a small ivory frill follows the upper bust line.

The neckline should frame her collarbones and face without becoming more visually dominant than her expression. The torso remains semi-fitted, while the lower dress widens into a short A-line skirt.

As Hayoon turns, create a shallow diagonal fold along the right side of the waist. The neckline and frill remain stable over the chest, and both straps remain visibly attached.

The skirt must not expand into a complete circle. Only one side of the hem rises approximately 5–8cm because of rotational inertia, while the opposite side remains closer to the legs. The ivory piping follows the actual curved hem as one continuous line.

The movement must not expose underwear or reveal an excessive amount of the interior lining. The frill stays physically attached to the neckline instead of floating away from the body.

Pair the dress with charcoal-black ribbed socks ending below the knees and substantial black leather Mary Jane sneakers or low platform shoes. The socks should show natural compression wrinkles, and the shoes must maintain believable contact with the floor.

## 8) Facial Expression

Hayoon’s upper eyelids lower slightly as her gaze moves, but her eyes remain visibly open and sharply readable. Maintain subtle natural tension around the lower eyelids and aegyo-sal so her expression does not become sleepy or empty.

Her forehead, eyebrows, cheeks and jaw stay relaxed. Her lips remain gently closed with neutral corners.

The expression should suggest that her attention has moved inward while her body continues turning. The beauty portrait should capture a brief thoughtful withdrawal rather than a conventional confident fashion expression.

She must not smile at the camera, appear dramatically melancholic or adopt a seductive sideways glance.

## 9) Lighting, Skin Tone and Atmosphere

Use a large diffused window source positioned behind and to camera-left at approximately 40–45°.

The light should preserve clarity on Hayoon’s face even while the body turns. Create a soft luminous plane along the left forehead, cheekbone, upper lip, jawline and shoulder, while retaining gentle shadow depth on the opposite side of the face.

Allow a thin natural light edge along the outer hair contour, left shoulder, upper arm and moving skirt hem. Use weak warm-neutral reflection from the cream wall to illuminate the right cheek, right side of the torso and deeper waist folds.

Let the pale floor return a subtle neutral-beige reflection onto the lower legs. The daylight should reveal the moving black fabric without turning it gray.

Avoid obvious studio flash, harsh beauty lighting or artificial rim light. Preserve accurate contact shadows beneath the grounded left shoe and the moving right forefoot.

## 10) Essential Beauty Direction and Physical Anchors

Hayoon’s face, eyes and skin must remain visually stable and recognizable while her dress and handbag convey movement around her. The portrait should not sacrifice facial anatomy or beauty detail for exaggerated action.

The central physical sequence is that the body changes direction first, the weighted skirt follows second and the handbag follows slightly later.

Maintain believable rotational inertia, weighted skirt motion, delayed handbag swing, realistic shoulder-strap tension and coherent foot and knee direction. Preserve complete contact of the left pivot foot, accurate floor shadows, natural waist compression and progressive optical depth.

Do not interpret movement as general image blur. Hayoon’s eyes, facial features, hands, straps, knees and room geometry must remain clearly formed.

## 11) Watermark

Add a small subtle watermark reading “ⓒyulim_mood” in the bottom-right corner. Use Helvetica Neue in a thin or light weight with white semi-transparent lettering.

Blend it softly into the photograph and maintain comfortable spacing from the frame edges. Do not overlap it with Hayoon’s moving foot, socks, handbag or the foreground chair.

## 12) Negative Prompt

# CUT 03 Prompt — The Frill Holds the Light

## 1) Beauty Editorial Direction, Film Reference and Color Palette

Build the image around three clearly separated tonal stages: Hayoon’s naturally luminous skin, the softly bright ivory frill and the deep matte black dress. Her face must remain the first point of attention, followed by the ivory neckline and then the dark dress.

She has large clear dark-brown almond-shaped eyes with soft natural double eyelids, subtle aegyo-sal and gently lifted outer eye corners. Her nose is small and straight with a softly rounded tip, and her lips are naturally full with a muted soft-pink tone.
